Title: Biosynthesis of the Plant-Produced Toxin Strychnine Elucidated

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geissoschizine toward dehydropreakuammicine, observed as formation of the deformylated product akuammicine in transient expression assays.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early conversion from geissoschizine toward Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue-specific RNA-seq/metabolomics candidate discovery; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; LC-MS metabolite analysis after geissoschizine infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of geissoschizine into the akuammicine/dehydropreakuammicine branch en route to strychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a CrGO homolog from the S. nux-vomica transcriptome. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana with geissoschizine produced akuammicine. 3. Establishes the first characterized oxidative step toward strychnine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid that antagonistically binds glycine and acetylcholine receptors; historically used as a rodent pesticide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified by co-expression in strychnine-producing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstruction and engineering of the early strychnine pathway in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named based on homology to Catharanthus roseus CrGO; observed product reflects spontaneous deformylation of an oxidized intermediate.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the dehydropreakuammicine ester moiety, permitting decarboxylation and downstream spontaneous deformylation to yield norfluorocurarine.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Intermediate conversion from dehydropreakuammicine to norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis using SnvGO as bait; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS detection of norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates formation of the decarboxylated intermediate norfluorocurarine en route to strychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of five α/β hydrolase candidates. 2. Transient co-expression supported formation of norfluorocurarine. 3. Functions in the conversion of dehydropreakuammicine-derived intermediate toward the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde route.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid that antagonistically binds glycine and acetylcholine receptors; rodenticide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SnvGO in strychnine-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be included in heterologous pathway assembly for strychnine intermediate production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SnvNS2 showed similar activity, indicating possible redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the dehydropreakuammicine ester moiety, permitting decarboxylation to form norfluorocurarine.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Intermediate conversion from dehydropreakuammicine to norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis using SnvGO as bait; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS detection of norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates formation of norfluorocurarine en route to strychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified among α/β hydrolase candidates. 2. Along with SnvNS1, supported norfluorocurarine formation in transient assays. 3. Represents an alternative enzyme for this hydrolytic/decarboxylative step.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid that antagonistically binds glycine and acetylcholine receptors; rodenticide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SnvGO in strychnine-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative catalyst for pathway engineering of strychnine intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally similar to SnvNS1 in the assay; redundancy not resolved.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late midstream oxidation toward Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to the next step leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with SnvGO and SnvNS1; LC-MS detection of 18-OH norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the hydroxylated intermediate required for Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified from CYP450 candidates co-expressed with SnvGO. 2. Co-expression produced 18-OH norfluorocurarine. 3. Defines the hydroxylation step preceding Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid acting on neuronal receptors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient combinatorial co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SnvGO in strychnine-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required oxidative module for heterologous reconstitution of the strychnine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of five CYP450 candidates screened.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces/rearranges the hydroxylated intermediate 18-OH norfluorocurarine to form the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late midstream conversion to Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor branch leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with SnvGO, SnvNS1, and SnvNO; LC-MS showing consumption of 18-OH norfluorocurarine and formation of Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes enzymatic formation of the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde intermediate en route to strychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified among MDR candidates. 2. Co-infiltration consumed 18-OH norfluorocurarine and produced the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde. 3. Establishes the final enzymatic step before acylation-mediated prestrychnine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid acting on neuronal receptors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient combinatorial co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SnvGO-linked candidates in strychnine-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for heterologous synthesis of the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four MDR candidates screened.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the predicted N-acylation of the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde-derived intermediate to complete diaboline biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Diaboline biosynthesis / branch related to strychnine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Diaboline branch from the shared Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics with strychnine-nonproducer Strychnos sp.; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs flux from the shared intermediate toward diaboline rather than strychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified from the nonproducing Strychnos sp. transcriptome as a BAHD acyltransferase candidate. 2. Transient co-expression completed diaboline biosynthesis. 3. Supports a branch point from the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shunts metabolic flux away from strychnine production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering alternative Strychnos alkaloid branch products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a strychnine-pathway enzyme per se, but a core branch enzyme discussed in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes N-acylation/malonylation of the Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde-derived intermediate to form prestrychnine.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acylation step leading to prestrychnine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog identification from S. nux-vomica transcriptome; transient co-expression with SnvGO, SnvNS1, SnvNO, SnvWS, and Arabidopsis thaliana malonyl-CoA synthetase in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces prestrychnine, the immediate precursor that spontaneously converts to strychnine/isostrychnine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a homolog of SpAT in S. nux-vomica. 2. Co-expression generated a malonylated product, prestrychnine. 3. Defines the last identified enzymatic step prior to spontaneous strychnine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotoxic alkaloid acting on glycine and acetylcholine receptors; rodenticide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Geissoschizine infiltration; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; co-expression with Arabidopsis thaliana malonyl-CoA synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific RNA-seq paired with metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana malonyl-CoA synthetase (co-expressed support enzyme)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key late-pathway enzyme for engineered strychnine or derivative production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prestrychnine subsequently converted spontaneously to strychnine and isostrychnine under assay conditions.
